objective: Close GAP-007 by making architecture warning ownership deterministic: unowned architecture warnings must fail the architecture gate unless explicitly accepted in .architecture-baseline.txt with owner, reason, valid UTC date, JSC ticket, and valid UTC expiry metadata.

observedWarnings.message: Auth-boundary command does not directly import node:crypto. Verify signing is delegated to a lib module that does.
Auth-boundary command does not directly import node:crypto. Verify signing is delegated to a lib module that does.
Auth-boundary command does not directly import node:crypto. Verify signing is delegated to a lib module that does.
Auth-boundary command does not directly import node:crypto. Verify signing is delegated to a lib module that does.

currentFinding.currentLoophole: The architecture gate returns pass when warnings are unbaselined, so the repo can accumulate architecture drift that is visible in JSON but not owned by a maintainer or blocking in CI.
currentFinding.desiredInvariant: A warning is either fixed, explicitly baselined with owner/reason/date/ticket/expires metadata, or treated as a failing architecture gate result.

intentReviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Baseline accepted debt can become permanent because ownership metadata has no expiry or revalidation trigger.
intentReviewFindingsAddressed.response: The implementation contract now requires JSC ticket and valid UTC expires metadata, and expired or malformed baselines must fail architecture validation instead of remaining silent debt.

intentReviewFindingsAddressed.finding: The intent validation gate references src/dev/check-architecture-rules-script.test.ts before the file exists.
intentReviewFindingsAddressed.response: The test file is intentionally created by this slice; the implementation approach now says to create it as the first code artifact and run it before claiming the gate.

intentReviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Acceptance criteria should explicitly require non-zero exit behavior for unowned warnings across output modes, not only JSON status semantics.
intentReviewFindingsAddressed.response: Acceptance criteria now require non-zero exit and fail status regardless of output format.

assumptions: The four current auth-commands-use-crypto warnings are either accepted delegated-auth technical debt or should be fixed before they can pass architecture validation.

Because this slice is about ownership enforcement, adding explicit baseline entries with owner/reason/valid UTC date/JSC ticket/valid UTC expires is acceptable only if those entries make the warning count non-blocking and visible as time-bounded baselined debt.
Failing unowned warnings is less broad and more repo-native than introducing dependency-cruiser or madge in this slice.
This slice does not prove a full import graph contract; it ratchets the existing custom architecture validator.
nonGoals: Do not introduce a new architecture tool or package dependency.
Do not refactor the four auth-boundary command files in this slice; if baseline ownership is deemed unacceptable, stop and create a separate delegated-auth refactor slice.
Do not change runtime evidence, review-state, external-state, delivery-truth, or public packet schemas.
Do not broaden GAP-007 into a full import graph or dependency-boundary implementation.
Do not claim the entire architecture enforcement roadmap is complete from this slice alone.
implementationApproach: Add script-level behavior that treats any unbaselined warning as a failing architecture gate result.
Preserve existing error semantics and keep baseline metadata validation strict.
Update JSON and console output so status becomes fail when either errors or unowned warnings exist.
Create src/dev/check-architecture-rules-script.test.ts as the focused script regression test for this slice.
Add a focused script regression test that creates a temporary repository with one warning and no baseline, then proves the script exits non-zero with status fail.

Add a focused regression proving the same warning can pass only when .architecture-baseline.txt contains owner, reason, valid UTC date, JSC ticket, and non-expired valid UTC expires metadata for the exact rule and relative path.
Add a focused regression proving expired baseline entries fail the gate even when rule, path, owner, reason, date, and ticket are present.
Add focused regressions proving malformed date, malformed expires, and non-JSC ticket metadata fail the gate.
Add explicit baseline entries for the four currently observed auth-boundary warnings as time-bounded delegated-auth debt for this slice.
Run pnpm architecture:check and require zero unowned warnings before recording the slice receipt.
riskControls: Do not hide architecture drift by deleting warnings from the validator.
Do not convert all warning classes to permanent errors without an escape hatch; the baseline file is the reviewed owner-visible escape hatch.
Baseline entries must be exact to rule and relative file path so broad suppressions cannot mask unrelated future drift.
Baseline entries without owner, reason, valid UTC date, JSC ticket, and valid UTC expires must continue to fail.
Expired or malformed baseline entries must fail so accepted warning debt cannot silently become permanent.
If reviewers reject baselining the four existing warnings, stop and record a blocker or split out a delegated-auth refactor rather than forcing a pass.
acceptanceCriteria: An unbaselined architecture warning causes scripts/check-architecture-rules.cjs to exit non-zero and report status fail regardless of output format.

A warning with an exact baseline entry containing owner, reason, valid UTC date, JSC ticket, and non-expired valid UTC expires metadata is reported as baselined rather than warning and does not fail the gate.
Invalid baseline metadata still fails the gate.
Expired or malformed baseline metadata fails the gate.
pnpm architecture:check passes in this checkout with zero unowned warnings.
The slice receipt records GAP-007, audit hash, validation commands, skill-lens checks, and independent reviewer artifacts.
The implementation notes and goal state clearly distinguish this ratchet from a full import graph contract.
blockedConditions: Stop if intent reviewers determine that baselining the four existing warnings would institutionalize an unsafe auth boundary.
Stop if the architecture script cannot be tested in a temporary repo without broad repo rewrites.
Stop if a package/tooling change becomes required; that exceeds this slice.
rollbackPath: Remove the PU-024 baseline entries from .architecture-baseline.txt if they are rejected.
Keep the intent and review artifacts as evidence of the attempted GAP-007 ratchet and record the blocker in receipts.jsonl.
